**Agent‑C – Development Estimate & Resource Plan**

Below is a high‑level, realistic estimate for building a *minimum‑viable food‑delivery app* (iOS + Android) that meets a 3‑month deadline and a €50 k budget.  
All figures are based on a **small, in‑house team** (or a low‑cost offshore partner) and assume that we keep the scope to the *core* set of features that deliver real business value in a city of ~500 k residents.

---

## 1. Feature Set – MVP (Must‑Have)

| Feature | Description | Priority |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| **User registration / login** (email, phone, social) | Standard authentication, password reset | High |
| **Restaurant catalog** | List, filter, search, basic details, menu items | High |
| **Cart & checkout** | Add/remove items, quantity, total, promo code | High |
| **Payment** | In‑app payment (Stripe/PayPal) *or* “cash on delivery” | Medium |
| **Order status & real‑time tracking** | Status updates (accepted, preparing, out for delivery, delivered) | Medium |
| **Delivery partner interface** | Separate lightweight UI for drivers (accept order, mark status) | Medium |
| **Admin dashboard** | CRUD for restaurants, menus, users, orders | High |
| **Push notifications** | Order status, promotions | Medium |
| **Basic analytics** | Daily active users, orders, revenue | Low |
| **Localization** | English + local language(s) | Medium |
| **Accessibility** | VoiceOver / TalkBack compliance | Low |

> **Why this scope?**  
> • Keeps the core value‑add (ordering & delivery) intact.  
> • Eliminates high‑cost features like advanced real‑time tracking, AI‑based recommendations, or a full‑blown restaurant‑management portal.  
> • Allows us to finish within 12 weeks and stay close to the €50 k budget.

---

## 2. Resource Allocation

| Role | # of People | Weekly Hours | Cost (€/hr) | Total Cost |
|------|-------------|--------------|-------------|------------|
| **iOS Developer** | 1 | 35 | 35 | €14 500 |
| **Android Developer** | 1 | 35 | 35 | €14 500 |
| **Backend Engineer (Node.js + PostgreSQL)** | 1 | 30 | 35 | €12 750 |
| **UX/UI Designer** | 0.5 | 20 | 35 | €2 800 |
| **QA / Test Engineer** | 0.5 | 15 | 35 | €2 100 |
| **Project Manager** | 0.25 | 15 | 35 | €1 650 |
| **DevOps / Ops** | 0.25 | 10 | 35 | €1 100 |
| **Contingency (10%)** | — | — | — | €3 850 |
| **Total** | — | — | — | **≈ €50 000** |

*Notes:*

- **Hourly rate** is based on a **mid‑range offshore team** (e.g., Eastern Europe).  
- **Time‑sharing** (part‑time) roles keep overhead low.  
- **Contingency** covers unplanned bugs, scope creep, or slight over‑runs.

---

## 3. Timeline (12 Weeks)

| Week | Milestone | Deliverable |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| **1–2** | *Kick‑off & Design* | Wireframes, UI kit, technical architecture |
| **3** | *Backend API (auth, restaurant CRUD, order flow)* | REST endpoints, database schema |
| **4–5** | *iOS & Android – Core Features* | Registration, catalog, cart |
| **6** | *Payment Integration* | Stripe/PayPal SDK, “cash on delivery” flow |
| **7** | *Delivery Partner UI* | Order acceptance, status updates |
| **8** | *Push Notifications* | Firebase Cloud Messaging

**Agent-F: Strategic Prioritization & Risk Mitigation**  

Let’s synthesize the inputs and align on a structured approach to maximize ROI within constraints. Here’s how we can move forward:  

---

### **1. Finalize MVP Scope**  
**Core Features (Non-Negotiable):**  
- **User Flow:** Registration, login, profile (Agent-D)  
- **Restaurant Catalog:** Search/filter, menus, ordering (Agent-B)  
- **Payment Gateway:** Stripe/Adyen (Agent-C)  
- **Order Tracking:** Real-time status updates (Agent-C)  
- **Admin Portal:** Restaurant onboarding, order management (Agent-B)  

**Nice-to-Have (Post-MVP):**  
- Loyalty programs  
- AI-driven recommendations  
- Advanced restaurant analytics  

**Why This Focus?**  
- **Cost Efficiency:** Avoid over-engineering for a 3-month timeline.  
- **User Retention:** Core ordering and payment must be flawless for adoption.  
- **Scalability:** Post-Launch, iterate on “nice-to-have” features based on feedback.  

---

### **2. Leverage Existing Solutions**  
**Third-Party Integrations (To Save Time/Money):**  
- **Payment Processing:** Stripe (Agent-C) for secure, low-fee transactions.  
- **Real-Time Tracking:** Use Mapbox or Google Maps APIs (Agent-B) instead of custom geolocation.  
- **Push Notifications:** Firebase Cloud Messaging (Agent-C) for cost-effective cross-platform support.  

**Example Cost Savings:**  
- Using Stripe + Mapbox reduces backend dev hours by 20% (vs. custom solutions).  

---

### **3. Address Risks Head-On**  
**Key Risks

Agent-E (Operations / Launch-readiness) – quick input:

1. City-size reality check  
   - 500 k inhabitants ≈ 180-220 k households.  
   - Realistic first-year active-base: 5-7 % → 10-15 k monthly actives.  
   - Peak concurrent users at dinner window: ≈ 1 500.  
   → The infra numbers from Agent-C (Node + RDS on AWS/DO) easily hold; no need for micro-services yet.

2. Restaurant supply side (make-or-break for adoption)  
   - Must sign 60-80 outlets before go-live to avoid “empty-shelf” problem.  
   - Offer “zero-fee first 3 months” + free photo shoot + self-onboarding portal (already in scope).  
   - Allocate €2 k from contingency for a part-time city hunter (can be a local student) – still inside €50 k ceiling.

3. Delivery fleet options inside MVP  
   - Option-A (leanest): use restaurants’ own riders → only need status toggle in restaurant portal, no driver app.  
   - Option-B (hybrid): crowd-source drivers with a Web-Progressive page instead of native driver app; cuts 2-3 dev weeks.  
   - Real-time GPS tracking can be postponed to Phase-2; SMS/WhatsApp status links keep users informed for now.

4. Compliance / quick wins  
   - GDPR: add “data export/delete” endpoint to admin panel (one day of backend work).  
   - PCI-DSS scope stays with Stripe/PayPal so we don’t need extra audits.  
   - Insurance: rider insurance is restaurants’ liability under Option-A; still mention it in T&Cs to avoid surprises.

5. Launch roadmap (inside 3-month window)  
   - Week-10: private beta with 10 restaurants, 100 test orders.  
   - Week-11: marketing landing page + referral code engine (adds ½ week to backend, already covered in buffer).  
   - Week-12: App-store submission (both stores), PR with local press, coupon “first order €5 off” capped at €500 promo budget (comes out of ops, not dev).

6. Post-launch hand-off package (so client can run without us)  
   - Run-book (deploy, rollback, DB backup, rotate keys).  
   - One-hour training video for restaurant managers.  
   - 30-day bug-fix warranty; afterwards move to T&M support if desired.

Bottom line: if we keep delivery logistics light and treat restaurant onboarding as a parallel work-stream, the MVP is doable inside 12 weeks / €50 k without major technical or regulatory roadblocks.
